headline: No PRs merged in pre-commit/pre-commit between May 5, 2026 and May 11, 2026.
definition: Portfolio flow efficiency: the share of cycle time that was actively worked on, vs. waiting in review or other queues. Portfolio FE = Σ active / Σ cycle across all merged PRs in this window — Vacanti's recipe, the right number to act on.
key_insight: An empty window means nothing to measure — not a bad signal.
next_actions: Confirm the repo name is correct.
caveats: Never use this metric per engineer — it reflects the system, not individuals.
Per-PR FE is a directional indicator, not a precise measurement.
Active time uses gap=4.0h and min_cluster=30.0min — tuning these moves the number.

vocabulary.Cycle time: Wall-clock time from when a PR was opened until it was merged. The clock starts at `created_at` and stops at `merged_at`.
vocabulary.Active time: The share of cycle time covered by clusters of activity events (commits, reviews, comments). Events more than `gap-hours` apart form separate clusters; each cluster credits at least `min-cluster-minutes` of active time.
vocabulary.Wait time: cycle_time − active_time. Time the PR spent waiting in queues (awaiting review, blocked, etc.). Vacanti's actionable signal — queues are where the system bottlenecks live.
vocabulary.Flow efficiency: active_time / cycle_time. Reported per-PR and as a portfolio.
vocabulary.Portfolio flow efficiency: Σ active / Σ cycle across all merged PRs in this window. Vacanti's system-level recipe — long-running PRs dominate, which is what you want.

cli_invocation: uv run flow efficiency week --repo pre-commit/pre-commit --start 2026-05-05 --stop 2026-05-11 --gap-hours 4.0 --min-cluster-minutes 30.0
